description: Bosniak v2019 CT test data covering all categories, the >25% solid-component exclusion, and category changes from the 2005 implementation.

testCases.name: Bosniak I - Simple Benign Cyst
testCases.clinicalContext: Homogeneous -9 to 20 HU simple fluid mass with thin smooth wall, no septa, no calcification, and no nodule.

testCases.name: Bosniak II - Few Thin Septa
testCases.clinicalContext: Thin smooth wall with 1-3 thin septa. Wall or septa may enhance in Bosniak II under v2019.

testCases.name: Bosniak II - Homogeneous High-Attenuation Cyst
testCases.changedFrom2005: 2005 implementation classified high attenuation >20 HU as IIF.
testCases.clinicalContext: Homogeneous >=70 HU noncontrast CT renal mass, non-enhancing and otherwise benign.

testCases.name: Bosniak II - Calcifications of Any Morphology
testCases.changedFrom2005: The retired implementation upgraded thick/nodular calcifications to III.
testCases.clinicalContext: Any calcification morphology is Bosniak II under v2019 if other features remain assessable.

testCases.name: Bosniak IIF - Many Thin Enhancing Septa

expectedOutputs.management: Follow-up imaging at 6 months, 12 months, then annually for 5 years

testCases.clinicalContext: Many (>=4) thin smooth enhancing septa meet the v2019 IIF criterion.

testCases.name: Bosniak IIF - Smooth 3 mm Enhancing Wall

expectedOutputs.management: Follow-up imaging at 6 months, 12 months, then annually for 5 years

testCases.clinicalContext: Smooth minimally thickened 3 mm enhancing wall meets v2019 IIF criteria.

testCases.name: Bosniak III - Thick Enhancing Wall
testCases.clinicalContext: Enhancing smooth thickened wall >=4 mm meets v2019 Bosniak III criteria when no enhancing nodule is present.

testCases.name: Bosniak III - Irregular Enhancing Septa
testCases.clinicalContext: Irregular enhancing septal thickening with obtuse margins and <=3 mm convex protrusion meets v2019 III criteria.

testCases.name: Bosniak IV - Enhancing Acute-Margin Nodule

expectedOutputs.term: Cystic mass, highly suspicious for malignancy

testCases.clinicalContext: Any enhancing convex protrusion with acute margins is a v2019 nodule and therefore Bosniak IV.

testCases.name: Not Applicable - >25% Enhancing Solid Tissue

expectedOutputs.term: Not a Bosniak-classifiable cystic renal mass
expectedOutputs.management: Evaluate as a solid renal mass with cystic or necrotic change

testCases.clinicalContext: Bosniak v2019 defines cystic renal masses as having less than approximately 25% enhancing tissue.

references.citation: Silverman SG, Pedrosa I, Ellis JH, et al. Bosniak Classification of Cystic Renal Masses, Version 2019: An Update Proposal and Needs Assessment. Radiology. 2019;292(2):475-488.
Bosniak MA. The current radiological approach to renal cysts. Radiology. 2005;236(1):61-70.
references.description: Prior CT-based classification retained as historical comparator
